APPEARANCES:
Christopher W. Wood of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4140), appearing for defendant / moving party Certainteed Corporation
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Jerry & Judith Cates

RULING:
DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as issued on 3/7/2013: Defendant Certainteed Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 4 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium), granted as to issue 5 (punitive damages) and off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2 and 4, defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of substantial factor causation. Dr. Bruch's declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to Certainteed's asbestos cement pipe was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Cates' lung cancer. As to summary adjudication issue 5, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation cause of action. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of substantial factor causation. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Cates' asbestos-related illness. The court notes that on the exposure issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cates was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Cates' deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs' request for a 437c(h) continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, and extending discovery cutoff. Preference is granted as to CCP 36 (a) only. Trial is March 11, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is March 7, 2013. All summary motions to be filed on regular notices, without the additional two days for e-service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than February 15, 2012. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on February 28, 2013. Time to respond to written contention discovery is shortened to 20 days. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 22, 2013. Plaintiffs will have until 11/16, Friday to produce the name of a witness, or this witness will be barred from testifying. Either side can bring a motion re: the witness if good cause shown. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 8, 2013. No later than Dec. 7, 2012, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than Dec. 7, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on January 9, 2013 at 1:30pm. In Department 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. The mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 is set on March 8, 2013 at 10:00 a.m. in Department 503. All counsel attending the conference must have knowledge about the fact of the case, and be present with the representatives who have authority to settle. Any person(s) need to appear by phone must seek approval by the Court prior to the conference. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 regarding the submission of settlement conference statements. The statements will be due by 4:00 p.m. on March 7, 2013. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
